title: Device sequence in multi-device test runs
body:
## Environment variables

DEVICE_SEQ is the integer index of the current device within a task. It starts at 0, so a task with 10 devices assigns DEVICE_SEQ values 0 through 9. Use DEVICE_SEQ to tell devices apart in a multi-device run.

title: Pipeline template migration guide
body:
## Migration parameters

LogicType selects the logic to run; the default is Change, which edits the json file, while Compare produces a parameter file. Channel is the target directory name. sourceFileName is the source json file to convert. Double-check these before running the pipeline.

title: Download retry postmortem
body:
## Root cause

Downloads failed on mobile networks because the retry path passed a stale context. Fix: pass the current context and add a bounded retry with backoff. Test improvement: add a non-wifi download case to the release checklist.

title: Retrieval architecture overview
body:
## Hybrid retrieval

Queries run through BM25-lite sparse retrieval and dense vector retrieval in parallel. The two ranked lists are fused with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) and an optional cross-encoder reranker selects the final candidates.

title: Inspecting task logs
body:
## Logs

In a load test, copy the device id of a failing device, search for it, and download its log file to investigate. Logs record per-device results keyed by DEVICE_SEQ.
